tasks.subject: [permission-prompt-survives-session-switch] [P1] Add startedAt + snapshot to PendingInteraction and populate in interactive-handlers
tasks.description:
Make every `PendingInteraction` self-describing so its client event can be rebuilt without the live SDK closure.

FILE: apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/messaging/interactive-handlers.ts

1) Extend the three union members (`PendingApproval`, `PendingQuestion`, `PendingElicitation`, ~lines 52-78) with two new fields each:
   - `startedAt: number` — server epoch ms when the interaction began.
   - `snapshot: <PerTypeSnapshot>` — a plain, serializable object equal to the original SSE event `data` MINUS the routing id, so a recovery path can re-emit the native event.
   Define per-type snapshot interfaces:
   - `ApprovalSnapshot { toolName: string; input: string; title?: string; displayName?: string; description?: string; blockedPath?: string; decisionReason?: string; hasSuggestions: boolean }`
   - `QuestionSnapshot { questions: QuestionItem[] }`
   - `ElicitationSnapshot { serverName: string; message: string; mode?: ...; url?: string; elicitationId?: string; requestedSchema?: Record<string, unknown> }`

2) Populate `startedAt` + `snapshot` at every `session.pendingInteractions.set(...)` call site:
   - `handleToolApproval` (~261-334): it already computes `const startedAt = Date.now()` at line 268 and builds the `approval_required` event `data`. Reuse that same `startedAt` and copy the same fields into `snapshot` (toolName, `input: JSON.stringify(input)`, title, displayName, description, blockedPath, decisionReason, `hasSuggestions: (context.suggestions?.length ?? 0) > 0`).
   - `handleAskUserQuestion` (~88-132): add `const startedAt = Date.now()` before the `eventQueue.push`, set `snapshot: { questions }`.
   - `handleElicitation` (~141-198): add `const startedAt = Date.now()`, set `snapshot: { serverName: request.serverName, message: request.message, mode: request.mode, url: request.url, elicitationId: request.elicitationId, requestedSchema: request.requestedSchema }`.

3) Do NOT change the in-band `eventQueue.push` payloads, the resolve closures, the reject closures, or the timeout/abort logic. `snapshot`/`startedAt` are pure additive metadata read only by the recovery selector (task 1.2).

Keep TSDoc on the exported `PendingInteraction` type accurate. This file already imports `QuestionItem` indirectly via `@dorkos/shared/types` (it imports `QuestionItem` at the top) — reuse it for the question snapshot.

ACCEPTANCE TESTS (add to apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/messaging/__tests__/interactive-handlers.test.ts, or create it):
- After calling `handleToolApproval(session, 'tc-1', 'Bash', { command: 'ls' }, ctx)`, `session.pendingInteractions.get('tc-1')` has `type: 'approval'`, a numeric `startedAt`, and a `snapshot` with `toolName: 'Bash'`, `input: '{"command":"ls"}'`, `hasSuggestions: false`. Purpose: snapshot is captured at registration without a live closure.
- `handleAskUserQuestion` registers a `snapshot.questions` deep-equal to the input questions. Purpose: question snapshot fidelity.
- `handleElicitation` registers a `snapshot.serverName`/`message` matching the request. Purpose: elicitation snapshot fidelity.
These are pure in-memory assertions — no SDK mock needed; the handlers operate on a plain `InteractiveSession` literal `{ pendingInteractions: new Map(), eventQueue: [] }`.
tasks.activeForm: Adding startedAt + snapshot to PendingInteraction and populating in interactive-handlers

tasks.subject: [permission-prompt-survives-session-switch] [P1] Surface getPendingInteractions on AgentRuntime, session-store, and runtime
tasks.description:
Expose the selector through the runtime abstraction so HTTP routes (Path A) and the stream handler (Path B) can read pending state without touching SDK internals directly.

1) packages/shared/src/agent-runtime.ts — add to the `AgentRuntime` interface, near the other interactive methods (`approveTool`/`submitAnswers`/`submitElicitation`, ~264-303):
```ts
/**
 * List the session's currently-pending interactions as recovery DTOs.
 *
 * Returns server-authoritative snapshots with remainingMs, excluding expired
 * (remainingMs <= 0) entries. Returns [] for an unknown or interaction-free
 * session. Read-only — never resolves or mutates an interaction.
 *
 * @param sessionId - Target session
 * @returns Recovery DTOs for every non-expired pending interaction
 */
getPendingInteractions(sessionId: string): PendingInteractionDTO[];
```
The DTO type must be importable from shared. Since `PendingInteractionDTO` is defined server-side in task 1.2, define the DTO's PUBLIC shape as a Zod schema + inferred type in packages/shared (see task 1.4) and import THAT here, keeping the server selector's return assignable to it. (SDK types must not leak into shared — the snapshot fields are plain JSON, so this is safe.)

2) apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/sessions/session-store.ts — add a method alongside `approveTool` (~248):
```ts
/** List non-expired pending interactions for a session (recovery selector). */
getPendingInteractions(sessionId: string): PendingInteractionDTO[] {
  const session = this.findSession(sessionId);
  if (!session) return [];
  return listPendingInteractions(session, Date.now());
}
```
Import `listPendingInteractions` from `../messaging/pending-interactions.js`.

3) apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/claude-code-runtime.ts — add the delegating method next to `approveTool` (~302) / `submitElicitation` (~317), matching the existing one-line delegation style:
```ts
getPendingInteractions(sessionId: string): PendingInteractionDTO[] {
  return this.sessionStore.getPendingInteractions(sessionId);
}
```

4) packages/test-utils/src/fake-agent-runtime.ts — add `getPendingInteractions = vi.fn<...>().mockReturnValue([]);` so `FakeAgentRuntime` still satisfies the interface (the project relies on this to fail-compile when the interface grows — see .claude/rules/testing.md). Also add it to packages/test-utils/src/mock-factories.ts mock object (`getPendingInteractions: vi.fn().mockReturnValue([])`).

Note: `DirectTransport`/Obsidian (apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/direct-transport.ts) calls the runtime in-process; a transport-level method is added in task 1.5 — this task only covers the runtime interface.

ACCEPTANCE TESTS (extend apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/__tests__ session-store tests, or a focused new test):
- `sessionStore.getPendingInteractions('unknown')` returns `[]` (no throw). Purpose: unknown-session safety.
- After registering a pending approval via the handler, `getPendingInteractions(sessionId)` returns one DTO. Purpose: delegation wiring.
- TypeScript compiles: `FakeAgentRuntime` and `createMockTransport` still satisfy their interfaces. Purpose: interface-growth fail-compile guard holds.
tasks.activeForm: Surfacing getPendingInteractions on AgentRuntime, session-store, and runtime

tasks.subject: [permission-prompt-survives-session-switch] [P1] Add remainingMs to shared event schemas and a PendingInteractionDTO schema
tasks.description:
Add the server-authoritative `remainingMs` field to the shared event schemas so re-emitted events (Path B) carry it, and define the Path A DTO schema in @dorkos/shared.

FILE: packages/shared/src/schemas.ts

1) Add an optional `remainingMs` to the three interaction event schemas (optional so the originating in-band emit, which omits it, still validates):
   - `ApprovalEventSchema` (~307-322): add `remainingMs: z.number().optional().describe('Server-authoritative ms left before auto-deny; present on recovery re-emit so the countdown resumes without resetting')`.
   - `QuestionPromptEventSchema` (~326-331): add the same optional `remainingMs`. (It currently has only `toolCallId` + `questions`; also add `startedAt: z.number().optional()` so a recovered question can seed a countdown consistently with approvals.)
   - `ElicitationPromptEventSchema` (~752-763): add the same optional `remainingMs` (and `startedAt: z.number().optional()` for parity).

2) Define the Path A DTO schema + type (place near the event schemas). A discriminated union by `type` mirroring the per-type fields plus `id`, `startedAt`, `remainingMs`:
```ts
export const PendingInteractionDTOSchema = z.discriminatedUnion('type', [
  z.object({ type: z.literal('approval'), id: z.string(), startedAt: z.number(), remainingMs: z.number(),
    toolName: z.string(), input: z.string(), title: z.string().optional(), displayName: z.string().optional(),
    description: z.string().optional(), blockedPath: z.string().optional(), decisionReason: z.string().optional(),
    hasSuggestions: z.boolean() }),
  z.object({ type: z.literal('question'), id: z.string(), startedAt: z.number(), remainingMs: z.number(),
    questions: z.array(QuestionItemSchema) }),
  z.object({ type: z.literal('elicitation'), id: z.string(), startedAt: z.number(), remainingMs: z.number(),
    serverName: z.string(), message: z.string(), mode: ElicitationModeSchema.optional(), url: z.string().optional(),
    elicitationId: z.string().optional(), requestedSchema: z.record(z.string(), z.unknown()).optional() }),
]).openapi('PendingInteractionDTO');
export type PendingInteractionDTO = z.infer<typeof PendingInteractionDTOSchema>;

export const PendingInteractionsResponseSchema = z.object({ interactions: z.array(PendingInteractionDTOSchema) }).openapi('PendingInteractionsResponse');
export type PendingInteractionsResponse = z.infer<typeof PendingInteractionsResponseSchema>;
```
`QuestionItemSchema` and `ElicitationModeSchema` already exist in this file. Ensure the new types are re-exported through `@dorkos/shared/types` / `@dorkos/shared/schemas` barrels (mirror how `ApprovalEvent` is exported).

3) The server selector (task 1.2) `PendingInteractionDTO` return type should be made assignable to this shared type — prefer having the server import the shared type as the canonical contract and keep its local snapshot interfaces structurally compatible.

ACCEPTANCE TESTS (packages/shared/src/__tests__/schemas.test.ts or a new pending-interactions schema test):
- `PendingInteractionDTOSchema.parse({ type: 'approval', id: 'x', startedAt: 1, remainingMs: 2, toolName: 'Bash', input: '{}', hasSuggestions: false })` succeeds. Purpose: approval DTO shape valid.
- A question DTO and an elicitation DTO each parse. Purpose: all three branches valid.
- `ApprovalEventSchema.parse({ ...validApproval, remainingMs: 1234 })` succeeds AND parsing without `remainingMs` still succeeds. Purpose: remainingMs is additive/optional and back-compatible.
tasks.activeForm: Adding remainingMs to shared event schemas and a PendingInteractionDTO schema

tasks.subject: [permission-prompt-survives-session-switch] [P1] Make client interaction renderers idempotent (upsert by interaction id)
tasks.description:
Make the stream handlers upsert by interaction id so the two recovery paths can both fire without producing duplicate cards. This is the linchpin guarantee.

FILE: apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/stream/stream-tool-handlers.ts

Current state:
- `handleApprovalRequired` (~136-168) already upserts: `findToolCallPart(approval.toolCallId)` → `Object.assign(existing, ...)` else push. KEEP, but also read `approval.remainingMs` and prefer it when seeding the countdown: when present, store `approvalRemainingMs: approval.remainingMs` alongside `approvalStartedAt`, and ensure a re-fire updates these fields in place (the `Object.assign` already does). Confirm a second call with the same `toolCallId` does NOT push a second part.
- `handleQuestionPrompt` (~171-194) already upserts by `findToolCallPart(question.toolCallId)`. KEEP; add seeding of `remainingMs`/`startedAt` if present so a recovered question shows the same countdown.
- `handleElicitationPrompt` (~197-215) ALWAYS pushes a new `elicitation` part — THIS IS THE DEDUP BUG for recovery. Change it to upsert by `interactionId`: search `helpers.currentPartsRef.current` for an existing part with `type === 'elicitation' && part.interactionId === elicitation.interactionId`; if found, `Object.assign` the new fields (and `remainingMs`/`startedAt` when present); else push as today. Add a helper `findElicitationPart(interactionId)` to StreamHandlerHelpers if one does not exist (mirror `findToolCallPart`/`findBackgroundTaskPart` in stream-event-types.ts).

Idempotency must hold across BOTH a foreground in-band emit and a later recovery re-emit/pull carrying the same id — the part is updated, never duplicated.

Wire `approvalRemainingMs`/equivalent into the ToolApproval/Question/Elicitation countdown: apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/tools/ToolApproval.tsx (and the question/elicitation renderers) already drive a countdown from `timeoutMs`/`approvalStartedAt` (#138). When a server `remainingMs` is present, derive the deadline as `Date.now() + remainingMs` instead of `approvalStartedAt + timeoutMs`, so a reconnect resumes at the true offset and never resets. Keep the existing path when `remainingMs` is absent (the originating turn).

Note any new fields on the tool_call/elicitation part type in the part-type definitions (search for `interactiveType`, `approvalStartedAt`, `timeoutMs` in the chat part types file) and add `approvalRemainingMs?: number` etc. there. Respect FSD: this is all within layers/features/chat — no cross-feature model imports.

ACCEPTANCE TESTS (apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/__tests__/stream-event-handler-*.test.ts — add a new stream-event-handler-pending-recovery.test.ts):
- Calling `handleApprovalRequired` twice with the same `toolCallId` yields exactly ONE tool_call part with `status: 'pending'`. Purpose: approval dedup.
- Calling `handleElicitationPrompt` twice with the same `interactionId` yields exactly ONE elicitation part. Purpose: elicitation dedup (the fixed bug).
- An approval re-fire carrying `remainingMs` updates the part's countdown seed in place. Purpose: countdown resumes from server offset, no reset.
tasks.activeForm: Making client interaction renderers idempotent (upsert by interaction id)

tasks.subject: [permission-prompt-survives-session-switch] [P1] Add usePendingInteractions fetch-on-mount and wire into use-session-history
tasks.description:
Pull pending interactions on session mount and feed them through the idempotent renderers so a switch/refresh/open-backgrounded session restores its prompt.

1) NEW HOOK — apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/use-pending-interactions.ts:
```ts
/** Fetch & hydrate any server-side pending interactions for the active session (recovery Path A). */
export function usePendingInteractions(params: { sessionId: string | null; selectedCwd: string | null; ... }) { ... }
```
Use TanStack Query: `queryKey: ['pending-interactions', sessionId, selectedCwd]`, `queryFn: () => transport.getPendingInteractions(sessionId!, selectedCwd ?? undefined)`, `enabled: sessionId !== null && !isStreaming`, `staleTime` short. On `data`, iterate `interactions` and route EACH through the SAME idempotent handler used by the live stream — call into the chat stream event processor with a synthesized event per `type` (`approval_required` from an approval DTO, `question_prompt` from a question DTO, `elicitation_prompt` from an elicitation DTO), passing `remainingMs`/`startedAt` so the countdown seeds from the server offset. Do NOT build a parallel renderer — reuse `handleApprovalRequired`/`handleQuestionPrompt`/`handleElicitationPrompt` (or the event-dispatch entry that calls them) so dedup from task 1.6 applies.

2) WIRING — apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/use-session-history.ts. Call `usePendingInteractions` so it runs AFTER `initSession()` resets `currentParts` (session-chat-store.ts `initSession` ~193-197 is the drop point). CRITICAL ORDERING: subscribe to the sync stream FIRST (the `useSSEConnection(syncUrl, ...)` already in this hook), THEN let the pull resolve — so a live event arriving during the fetch is deduped by id, not missed. Since the pull is idempotent by id (task 1.6), order only affects which path paints first, never correctness.

3) The hook must re-run when `sessionId` changes (switch) and on mount (refresh/cold nav). Mount + session change both go through `initSession()`, so keying the query on `sessionId` and feeding results post-reset covers all three DOR-73 cases (switch, hard refresh, open-backgrounded).

Respect FSD: this hook lives in features/chat/model and may import entities/session + shared. Keep TSDoc on the exported hook.

ACCEPTANCE TESTS (apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/__tests__/use-chat-session-sync.test.tsx or a new use-pending-interactions.test.tsx, with mock Transport via createMockTransport):
- Mount with `transport.getPendingInteractions` resolving one approval DTO → exactly one pending approval card hydrates. Purpose: Path A hydrate-on-mount.
- The hydrated card's countdown is seeded from the DTO `remainingMs` (assert the part carries the server offset, not a fresh `timeoutMs`). Purpose: countdown seed from server.
- If a live `approval_required` for the same id arrives during/after the fetch, still exactly one card. Purpose: pull + live dedup (relies on 1.6).
tasks.activeForm: Adding usePendingInteractions fetch-on-mount and wiring into use-session-history

tasks.subject: [permission-prompt-survives-session-switch] [P1] Verify single-resolve guard returns benign no-op on stale approve/deny/respond
tasks.description:
Verify (and lock in with tests) that answering an already-resolved interaction is a safe no-op — never a double tool execution. This guards correctness when multiple recovery surfaces could each submit.

CURRENT STATE (confirmed in apps/server/src/routes/sessions.ts): POST `/approve` (~391-409), `/deny` (~411-430), `/submit-answers` (~466-485), `/submit-elicitation` (~487-506) all already call `runtime.approveTool`/`submitAnswers`/`submitElicitation`, and on a `false` return distinguish `409 INTERACTION_ALREADY_RESOLVED` (session still exists, interaction gone) vs `404 NO_PENDING_*` (session gone). The session-store resolve methods (session-store.ts ~248-291) look up the interaction id, and the handler's resolve closure deletes the entry from `pendingInteractions` on first resolve (interactive-handlers.ts ~314, ~113, ~186). So a second submit finds nothing → returns false → 409/404. This already matches the #145 Slack stale-click guard.

TASK: this is primarily verification + test-hardening, not new logic. Confirm no code change is needed by tracing the delete-on-resolve. If a gap exists (e.g., a route that resolves without the delete-then-guard), fix it minimally. Confirm the client treats 409 as a benign 'already handled' (the resolved card simply transitions via the incoming tool_result) and not as an error toast — check apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/transport/session-methods.ts `approveTool`/`denyTool` and ToolApproval.tsx error handling; if a 409 currently surfaces an error, swallow it as already-resolved.

ACCEPTANCE TESTS (apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/sessions-interactive.test.ts):
- First POST /approve for a pending id resolves it (200 ok). A SECOND POST /approve for the same id returns 409 `INTERACTION_ALREADY_RESOLVED` and the underlying SDK resolve is invoked exactly once (assert the FakeAgentRuntime/session-store resolve spy is called once). Purpose: no double tool-execution from a duplicate/stale answer.
- POST /deny then POST /approve for the same id: the second is 409, no second resolve. Purpose: cross-surface stale answer is inert.
- POST /approve for an unknown session returns 404. Purpose: post-restart safety.
CLIENT TEST: a 409 from approveTool does not raise a user-facing error; the card resolves on the subsequent tool_result. Purpose: benign UX on a raced answer.
tasks.activeForm: Verifying single-resolve guard returns benign no-op on stale approve/deny/respond

tasks.subject: [permission-prompt-survives-session-switch] [P2] Route re-emitted interaction events through the idempotent renderer in syncEventHandlers
tasks.description:
Ensure the re-emitted Path B events (which arrive on the persistent sync stream, NOT the in-band POST /messages stream) reach the same idempotent renderers.

FILE: apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/use-session-history.ts — `syncEventHandlers` (~158-187).

Today `syncEventHandlers` handles only `sync_update` and `presence_update`. `useSSEConnection` (apps/client/src/layers/shared/model/use-sse-connection.ts) dispatches by event name to `eventHandlers[type](parsedData)`. Add three handlers keyed by the native event names so the re-emitted prompts are processed:
```ts
approval_required: (data) => routeToChatRenderer({ type: 'approval_required', data }),
question_prompt: (data) => routeToChatRenderer({ type: 'question_prompt', data }),
elicitation_prompt: (data) => routeToChatRenderer({ type: 'elicitation_prompt', data }),
```
Each must feed the SAME idempotent handler used by the in-band stream (`handleApprovalRequired`/`handleQuestionPrompt`/`handleElicitationPrompt` from stream-tool-handlers.ts, via the chat stream event processor). Because the renderer upserts by id (task 1.6), a prompt already painted by the in-band turn or the Path A pull is updated in place, not duplicated.

This hook (use-session-history.ts) is in features/chat/model and does not currently hold the chat stream processor reference — wire the renderer entrypoint in via params (the same place use-chat-session composes use-session-history and the stream processor), or expose a small `applyRecoveredInteraction(event)` callback from the chat model and pass it down. Keep it within features/chat (no cross-feature model import — FSD).

Keep the existing `sync_update`/`presence_update` handlers unchanged; just add the three new keys to the `syncEventHandlers` `useMemo` and its dependency list.

ACCEPTANCE TESTS (apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/__tests__/use-chat-session-sync.test.tsx):
- Simulate the sync stream delivering an `approval_required` event (Path B) → a pending approval card renders. Purpose: Path B events are handled on the sync channel.
- Deliver the SAME `approval_required` id via both the Path A pull AND the Path B sync event → exactly one card. Purpose: cross-path dedup end-to-end.
- Deliver a Path B `elicitation_prompt` then a duplicate → one elicitation card. Purpose: elicitation Path B dedup.
tasks.activeForm: Routing re-emitted interaction events through the idempotent renderer in syncEventHandlers

tasks.subject: [permission-prompt-survives-session-switch] [P2] Resolve recovered cards on tool_result and on countdown-zero timeout
tasks.description:
Close the loop: a recovered card must resolve when its result/answer arrives, and must self-clear if its countdown reaches zero with no result (clock skew vs. the server auto-deny), so stale prompts never linger.

FILES: apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/stream/stream-tool-handlers.ts and the interaction renderers (ToolApproval.tsx + question/elicitation renderers under apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/tools/).

1) RESOLVE-ON-RESULT: `handleToolResult` (stream-tool-handlers.ts ~110-133) already sets a matching `tool_call` part to `status: 'complete'`. Confirm this also clears the `pending` interactive state for a RECOVERED approval/question part (recovered parts share the same `toolCallId`, so `findToolCallPart` matches). For elicitation: a recovered elicitation part is keyed by `interactionId`, not `toolCallId` — ensure the elicitation completion path (elicitation_complete event / answer submit) transitions the recovered elicitation part to resolved by `interactionId`. Add the match if missing.

2) COUNTDOWN-ZERO PROACTIVE TIMEOUT: in ToolApproval.tsx (and the question/elicitation renderers), the #138 countdown already ticks toward a deadline. When the deadline (now derived from server `remainingMs`, task 1.6) reaches zero AND no resolving event has arrived, transition the card to a local 'timed-out' state (disable the Approve/Deny buttons, show timed-out copy) — matching the existing #138 direction. This prevents a recovered-but-already-auto-denied prompt from showing live buttons after the server has moved on.

Do not change server timeout duration or the resolve routes. This is client presentation only.

ACCEPTANCE TESTS (apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/__tests__/ and a renderer test under ui/tools/__tests__/):
- A recovered pending approval part transitions to resolved/complete when a `tool_result` for its `toolCallId` arrives. Purpose: resolve-on-result for recovered cards.
- A recovered elicitation part resolves when its completion arrives keyed by `interactionId`. Purpose: elicitation resolution path.
- A ToolApproval card seeded with `remainingMs` near zero transitions to a timed-out state with disabled buttons when the countdown hits zero and no result arrives (use fake timers, no arbitrary setTimeout). Purpose: proactive timeout, no lingering stale prompt.
tasks.activeForm: Resolving recovered cards on tool_result and on countdown-zero timeout

tasks.subject: [permission-prompt-survives-session-switch] [P3] Server cross-cutting tests: endpoint, re-emit, selector boundary, single-resolve
tasks.description:
Consolidate and harden the server-side acceptance coverage in apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/sessions-interactive.test.ts and the selector/handler unit tests, ensuring the cross-cutting guarantees are pinned (beyond the per-task tests already added in Phase 1/2).

Write/verify these with supertest + FakeAgentRuntime (or a real session-store fixture where the selector math must be exercised end-to-end), each with a purpose comment per .claude/rules/testing.md, targeting real failure modes — not always-pass assertions:
1) GET /api/sessions/:id/pending-interactions: returns active interactions with `remainingMs`; EXCLUDES expired (`remainingMs <= 0`); 404 on unknown session; `{ interactions: [] }` when none. Purpose: Path A contract.
2) GET /:id/stream connect: pending interactions re-emitted as native `approval_required`/`question_prompt`/`elicitation_prompt` AFTER `sync_connected`, each with `remainingMs`; expired ones not re-emitted; no spurious events when none pending. Purpose: Path B contract.
3) listPendingInteractions selector: `remainingMs` math and the expiry boundary where `now - startedAt === INTERACTION_TIMEOUT_MS` is excluded (remainingMs === 0). Purpose: edge boundary.
4) Single-resolve: a second /approve for an already-resolved id is a benign no-op (409, underlying resolve called exactly once, no second tool execution); /deny-then-/approve inert; unknown session 404. Purpose: double-resolution safety.

Use `collectSseEvents` from @dorkos/test-utils for the SSE assertions. Avoid duplicating Phase 1/2 unit tests verbatim — this task owns the end-to-end HTTP/SSE acceptance shape and the boundary/safety matrix. Run with `pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/sessions-interactive.test.ts` and the messaging selector test; all green.
tasks.activeForm: Writing server cross-cutting tests for endpoint, re-emit, selector boundary, and single-resolve

tasks.subject: [permission-prompt-survives-session-switch] [P3] Client cross-cutting tests: idempotent upsert, hydrate+countdown, resolve-on-result
tasks.description:
Consolidate the client acceptance coverage across the chat model tests, pinning the dedup + hydration + resolution guarantees with mock Transport (createMockTransport via TransportProvider per .claude/rules/testing.md).

Target files: apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/__tests__/use-chat-session-sync.test.tsx, apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/__tests__/session-chat-store.test.ts, and the stream-event-handler-*.test.ts suite. Each test gets a purpose comment.
1) Idempotent upsert: handling the same `approval_required` id twice → one card; same for `question_prompt` and (the fixed) `elicitation_prompt` by `interactionId`. Purpose: core dedup guarantee across all three types.
2) usePendingInteractions hydrate-on-mount: mock `transport.getPendingInteractions` to resolve one approval DTO; after mount, one pending card exists with its countdown seeded from `remainingMs` (assert the server offset, not a fresh `timeoutMs`). Purpose: Path A hydration + countdown seed.
3) Cross-path dedup: same id delivered via Path A pull AND a Path B sync `approval_required` → exactly one card. Purpose: hybrid safety.
4) Resolve-on-result: a `tool_result` for a recovered pending id resolves/collapses the card; a countdown-to-zero with no result marks the card timed-out (fake timers — no arbitrary setTimeout). Purpose: resolution + no lingering stale prompt.
5) Store interaction with initSession: after `initSession()` resets `currentParts`, a subsequent recovery hydrate re-populates the pending part (session-chat-store.test.ts). Purpose: the literal switch/refresh drop-and-restore.

Run with `pnpm vitest run` on the affected client test files (verify via `pnpm test -- --run` for the DEV-env gotcha noted in project memory). All green.
tasks.activeForm: Writing client cross-cutting tests for idempotent upsert, hydration, and resolve-on-result

tasks.subject: [permission-prompt-survives-session-switch] [P3] Document the endpoint in /api/docs and the re-emit-on-connect behavior in contributing SSE notes
tasks.description:
Ship the documentation deliverables.

1) /api/docs entry for `GET /api/sessions/:id/pending-interactions`: ensure the OpenAPI registration added in task 1.5 produces a complete, accurate entry — path, `:id` param, `cwd` query param, 200 response `PendingInteractionsResponseSchema` ({ interactions: PendingInteractionDTO[] }), 404 `SESSION_NOT_FOUND`. Describe it as a read-only recovery pull that returns non-expired pending interactions with server-authoritative `remainingMs`. Verify it renders at `/api/docs` (the server exposes API docs there per AGENTS.md).
2) contributing/ SSE/streaming notes: add a short subsection (in the existing SSE/streaming or session-sync guide under contributing/ — find it via the streaming/ADR-0117 references; e.g. an architecture or data-fetching guide) describing the re-emit-on-connect behavior: on every `GET /:id/stream` (re)connect, the server replays non-expired pending interactions as their native `approval_required`/`question_prompt`/`elicitation_prompt` events (with `remainingMs`) immediately after `sync_connected`, and the client renderer is idempotent by interaction id. Reference ADR-0117 (the persistent SSE transport this extends) and note the hybrid pull (Path A) + re-emit (Path B) recovery model and the no-cross-restart-durability boundary.
3) If the candidate ADR from the spec ("Recover pending interactions via hybrid pull + SSE re-emit; keep pendingInteractions in-memory authoritative") is to be created, note it here for /adr:from-spec follow-up (decisions/0262-... already drafted per repo status — reconcile/reference it).

ACCEPTANCE: `/api/docs` shows the new endpoint with correct schema; a contributing guide documents re-emit-on-connect and references ADR-0117; cross-restart boundary stated.
